methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $224M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 13 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Echodyne, a Series C defense & security company that has raised $224M to date, was valued using two triangulation methods against a peer universe of 13 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, supplemented by two public benchmarks (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). The private peer set ranges from $300M to $9.2B, with notable comparables including Saronic ($9.2B, Series D), Chaos Industries ($4.5B, Series D), and Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI (each $1.4B–$1.5B, Series B).

The Stage-Progression method applied a 1.7x step-up multiplier—standard for Series C advancement—to the $224M total raised proxy, yielding $381M. The Peer-Median method anchored to the $1.2B median valuation across all 13 comparables, reflecting the distribution observed in the Defense & Security sector at similar funding stages. These two approaches produced a triangulated range of $324M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $815M.

The wide spread between methods reflects material stage and business maturity dispersion within the peer set. The Stage-Progression floor of $324M represents conservative baseline progression, while the Peer-Median ceiling of $1.2B–$1.4B assumes Echodyne performs comparably to established Series B/D peers. Key uncertainties include revenue scale relative to peers, technology defensibility within radar/sensing applications, and customer concentration in defense procurement cycles.
